## Parquet â†’ SQL â†’ PostgreSQL load path (`pg-load-path`)

**Status:** `measured`
**Report:** [`docs/benchmarks/2026-07-12-v1.md`](2026-07-12-v1.md)

Wall time on like-for-like PostgreSQL load is server-dominated; CPU is the clearer differentiator. Machine: Apple M3 laptop â€” relative evidence, not a cloud SLA.

| System | Metric | Value | Detail |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Pramen â†’ PostgreSQL (binary COPY) | `rows_out_per_s` | 434kâ€“581k | wall 6.9â€“9.2 s; CPU 1.2â€“1.4 s; ~0.5 GiB RSS (3 runs) |
| DuckDB â†’ PostgreSQL (postgres extension) | `rows_out_per_s` | 403kâ€“620k | wall tie with Pramen; CPU 6.9â€“10.9 s; ~45 MiB RSS |
| DataFusion direct (no sink) | `rows_out_per_s` | ~4.0M | engine ceiling for the same SQL; exposes load-path cost |
| DuckDB â†’ native table (no PostgreSQL) | `rows_out_per_s` | ~8.2M | not a PostgreSQL load â€” cheapest possible destination |
